actions.intent: Break the crate and key ice together to reveal the rescue route.
Use a low-residue route-support clear near the bottom lane.
Build route access while keeping dock residue manageable.
Take a clean side triple to preserve dock room.
Continue opening the route after gravity refreshes the lane.
Use an upper clear to keep the dock from crowding the rescue sequence.
Clear safe upper material before committing to extraction.
Break the remaining crate pressure and stay ahead of water.
Stabilize the board after the water rise.
Finish the opened ice route and extract the puppy.

notes: Designer golden for ice-route practice: use the opened ice lane, keep dock stable, then finish the rescue.
